description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a symmetrical design, featuring two large, curved blades on either side of a central metallic spike. The blades are metallic with a slightly matte finish and have a beveled edge, giving them a sharp and robust appearance. The central spike protrudes above the blades, adding both a decorative and functional element. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and appears to be made of wood, capped at both ends with metallic fittings for reinforcement. The overall construction suggests durability and balance, suitable for both display and practical use. The color palette is dominated by muted metallic grays and natural wood tones, with no visible markings or engravings.
description: A double-headed battle axe with large metallic blades, a central spike, and a straight wooden handle capped with metal. The design is symmetrical and robust, with a matte metallic and wood finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large, curved blades and the central spike, with the full width of the axe visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the straight cylindrical handle, with the central spike visible at the top.
description_view_2: Rear view, identical to the front, displaying both blades and the central spike symmetrically.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and straight handle, mirroring view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle with metallic end caps, smooth surface, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical, large curved blades with beveled edges and a central metallic spike; no beard or poll, functional and decorative.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible metallic fittings at the top and bottom of the handle.
